title: narcissus autumnalis subsp. obsoletus
intro: Narcissus autumnalis subsp. obsoletus, also known as the autumn-flowering daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and ability to bloom in late summer to early fall. The medicinal properties of Narcissus autumnalis subsp. obsoletus include its alkaloid content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and muscular spasms. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to act as a natural sedative, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Narcissus autumnalis subsp. obsoletus can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, and ointments, which are often used to relieve muscle and joint pain, and to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
